# La Pong 2d - A simple pong game made with Godot Engine 4.6.1

## General rules
- Windows is default system for development
- Godot Engine 4.6.1 is the game engine used for development (Godot_v4.6.1-stable_win64.exe)
- main programming language is GDScript
- if unsure for certain topic, Always fetch information from official Godot documentation https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/ and Wikipedia https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
- ALWAYS respect DRY principle (Don't Repeat Yourself) and KISS principle (Keep It Simple, Stupid) when writing code. Avoid unnecessary complexity and duplication.
- Always write types for variables, function parameters, and return values in GDScript to improve code readability and maintainability.
- Always add minimal but meaningful comments
- Always respect best practices for GDScript and Godot Engine development, such as using signals for communication between nodes, using groups for organizing nodes, and following the scene tree structure effectively.
- Always test the game after implementing new features or making changes to ensure that everything works as expected and that no new bugs have been introduced.

## Project structure
- `assets/`: Contains all game assets such as sprites, sounds, and music.
- `scenes/`: Contains all the Godot scene files (.tscn) for the game.
- `scripts/`: Contains all the GDScript files for game logic.
- `_export/`: Contains exported builds of the game (ignored in version control).
